name: aerocement
tagline: Lightweight foamed concrete with alkali-resistant fiber, mixable with a drill and a bucket.
description: AR-GFRC aerocement: open-source foamed concrete targeting >=3 MPa compressive / >=1.5 MPa flexural at <=1000 kg/m3, load-matched to single-story walls. Failures are documented on purpose.
intended_use: Low-cost, owner-built single-story walls and small structures in resource-constrained settings.
repo_description: Open-source lightweight foamed concrete (AR-GFRC) for single-story walls. Drill-and-bucket buildable.
health_safety: Cement is caustic: wear gloves and eye protection. Cut glass fiber irritates skin and lungs: wear a mask when handling dry fiber. Test batches only; no structural use until independently validated.
validated: Fiber content must stay <=1.5% to avoid foam collapse
E-glass degrades in alkali; use zirconia (AR) or basalt fiber
Low elastic modulus: suspended spans are impractical
theoretical: >=3 MPa compressive at <=1000 kg/m3 (target, not yet measured)
>=1.5 MPa flexural at <=1000 kg/m3 (target, not yet measured)

known_errors.title: H-003 radiative-capture headline overstated \~10x (likely decimal/units error)
known_errors.summary: The published H-003 thermal cascade headline figure for radiative capture appears inflated by roughly one order of magnitude due to a decimal or units slip. The physically realistic ceiling is far lower.
known_errors.resolution: A 1-2 m2 instrumented test at Sikeston climate per the three written protocols, with raw data published win or lose. Until then: do not cite the headline number anywhere.
